summary: Full-stack AI-powered Resume Analyzer module (part of a larger AI career system) with Python/FastAPI backend, NLP processing (spaCy, Sentence Transformers/TF-IDF), ML-based job role prediction, and Streamlit frontend. Features: resume upload (PDF/DOCX), ATS score generation with skill match percentage, skill gap analysis with missing skills list, career recommendations (projects, certifications, tools), job role prediction (top 3 with confidence percentages and explanation), and modular/scalable code structure with SQLite database.
architectural_notes: NLP stack: spaCy (NER), Sentence Transformers (semantic similarity), TF-IDF + Cosine Similarity fallback
ML stack: scikit-learn for job role classifier (Logistic Regression / Naive Bayes / BERT)
Resume Analyzer is a module within a larger AI career system — design for modularity and integration
Frontend: Streamlit dashboard (primary); Backend: Python with modular code structure

existing_features.title: Implement a resume upload interface that accepts PDF and DOCX files. Extract and display the raw text content from the uploaded file on the frontend.

Implement an ATS Score Generator on the backend. Accept the extracted resume text and a selected job role. Compare the resume against predefined job descriptions using TF-IDF and cosine similarity. Return a numeric ATS score from 0 to 100.

Implement Skill Gap Analysis on the backend. Extract skills from the resume text using keyword matching against a predefined skills dictionary for the selected job role. Return matched skills, missing skills, and skill gap percentage.

Implement a Career Recommendation Engine on the backend. Based on the missing skills for the selected job role, return a curated list of suggested projects to build, certifications to complete, and tools to learn.

Implement a Job Role Prediction feature on the backend. Without requiring the user to select a job role, analyze the resume text and return the top 3 most suitable job roles with confidence percentages using a pre-built or rule-based scoring model.

Implement Resume Improvement Suggestions on the backend. Analyze the resume text to detect weak or passive action verbs, and return suggestions for stronger alternatives along with generic formatting improvement tips.

Build a clean, organized dashboard UI with distinct sections for: Resume Upload, ATS Score (with gauge/meter), Skill Gap Analysis (bar chart), Job Role Prediction (bar chart), Career Recommendations, and Resume Improvement Suggestions. Apply a polished, professional visual theme with a dark or neutral color palette, modern typography, and card-based layout.

Update the Job Role Prediction frontend section to display the explanation for each of the top 3 predicted roles. For each role, show the list of detected skills and the matching factors beneath or alongside the existing confidence bar/chart visualization.

new_feature_requests.summary: Resume Upload: Accept PDF/DOCX, extract and preprocess text using NLP
ATS Score Generator: Compare resume to job role descriptions using semantic similarity, output score out of 100
Skill Gap Analysis: Extract skills via NER, compare with job role requirements, show matched/missing skills and gap percentage
Career Recommendation Engine: Suggest projects, certifications, and tools based on missing skills
Job Role Prediction: Predict top 3 suitable job roles with confidence percentages using a classification model
Resume Improvement Suggestions: Detect weak action verbs, suggest better bullet points and formatting improvements
UI Dashboard: ATS score meter, skill gap bar chart, job suitability prediction chart using Streamlit or React
ATS Feature: NLP preprocessing with stopword removal and lemmatization before similarity comparison
Job Role Prediction: Provide explanation for prediction including detected skills and matching factors
Modular folder structure with clean, scalable code for the Resume Analyzer module

Enhance the backend ATS score computation to include NLP preprocessing steps — stopword removal and lemmatization — on the resume text before performing TF-IDF cosine similarity comparison against the job description for the selected role. The preprocessed text should be used for all downstream scoring.

Enhance the Job Role Prediction backend logic to return, alongside the top 3 predicted roles and confidence percentages, an explanation for each prediction. The explanation must list the skills detected in the resume and the matching factors (keywords/skills) that contributed to that role's confidence score.
